name: North Ostrobothnia (Finland)

colors.symbolism: the azure field of the historic Ostrobothnian arms, evoking the lakes and skies of the region
the silver ermine spots of the arms

description: A square blue flag charged with six silver ermine spots arrayed 3-2-1 in the centre, the heraldic banner of the North Ostrobothnia coat of arms.
history: North Ostrobothnia (Pohjois-Pohjanmaa) became a region under Finland's 1997 administrative reform; its banner is the heraldic flag of the regional coat of arms, derived from the historic provincial arms of Ostrobothnia.

features.symbolism: six silver ermine spots arrayed 3-2-1 on the blue field, derived from the historic provincial arms of Ostrobothnia and referring to the medieval ermine-fur trade that gave the region its early prosperity.
